This is an **Android tombstone** crash from a .NET MAUI app (`com.companyname.MauiTest`). Here's my analysis:

## Crash Summary

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Signal | SIGABRT ("not compatible") |
| ABI | arm64 |
| App | com.companyname.MauiTest |
| Runtime | libmonosgen-2.0.so (Mono) |
| BuildId | `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be` |

## .NET Runtime Frames (frames #01–#07)

All are in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`:
```
#01 pc 0x222098
#02 pc 0x2241ac
#03 pc 0x1abc80
#04 pc 0x1abd14
#05 pc 0x16dfa4
#06 pc 0x16db6c
#07 pc 0x16de04
```

Frame #08 is JIT-compiled managed code (anonymous mapping).

Let me attempt to download debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server and symbolicate these frames.

The app called **`Environment.FailFast()`** with the message *"not compatible"* (visible in the abort message). The UTF-16 memory dump near x14 also shows *"some intentional crash on startup."*
